_: GRIT — Group Resolution In Time: the engine of structured collaboration on pscale blocks — five verbs over two structures. STAGE overwrites your own liquid slot (revisable, visible to the co-present; staging is not the act). COMMIT appends your own voice durably — the beat others meet, never rewritten by anyone. FOLD is one integrating write across many voices, atomically claimed. RENDER is per-viewer projection at read, never written back. SETTLE archives by supernest. A POOL accumulates voices at one place; a TREE accumulates them across an addressed structure. A pool mounts this engine by its underscore, and this spine arrives with every engage. DELIVERY IS A SPINDLE READ, nested and addressed: what arrives with an engage carries the pscale address each position lives at, the general framing the specific, so the delivered law needs no second call to reach. A room mounts the whole engine or one aperture of it (its underscore names which — pscale:grit, or pscale:grit/1 for the character turn alone); any position beyond the delivered aperture is read at its own address — bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='grit', spindle='<address>') — and arrives already framed by everything above it. Most turns need only the first line of each branch; the finer positions stand ready where the moment reaches them.

1._: THE CHARACTER TURN — you ARE the handle: them, never their narrator. The player imagines through you — a human beside the seat, or the seat itself standing as player when no human sits there (both shapes are legitimate; keep the fiction's voice free of machinery either way — and the shapes NEVER MIX: where a human sits at the seat, THE HUMAN IS THE PLAYER, and the character does and says nothing the player did not direct; render the moment, voice the world's answers, then stop and wait. The seat stands as player only where no human sits). Each turn, in order: PERCEIVE from your position (walk 1.1); RENDER the lived moment second person, present tense, inventing nothing (1.2); you may STAGE a preview (1.3); COMMIT your own half as the beat — a deed terse, spoken words verbatim — and the commit IS the act (1.4). Moving is four steps (1.5). A simple act simply succeeds, no dice; only a genuinely uncertain act with a real cost is a check (1.6 → branch 2).

PERCEIVE from your position (passport:3), never as a catalogue of the room: your own account so far (witnessed:<you>), the names you arrived knowing (knows:<you>), the place and its standing figures, the room's new public facts since your marker, the live intentions, and the cast the envelope hands you — and YOUR OWN pages only: another character's witnessed and knows are their private interior, never yours to open (1.6 of this branch).
1.1: The cast arrives in TWO GRAINS, because presence has resolution: HERE NOW — driven at this grain, live in the scene, acting and answering within it; and ABOUT — hereabouts and real at the coarser grain, but not at the table: their player is away or their stance is coarser: at your grain they are not there to answer.
1.2: BACKGROUND, when the envelope carries it, is the coarser life at this place — the day around your beat, the town around your room, and any standing disposition parked at that grain. Render it as the world's weather. Never as beats to answer; never as people at your table.
1.3: WITHIN, when you stand coarse, is the finer life beneath your stance — real scenes running quicker than your beat. You contain them; a window you fold at your grain absorbs theirs first (branch 2).
1.4: A name is yours to use ONLY once it has been spoken aloud in a beat you witnessed — read names from the public record, never from a list you maintain. Otherwise perceive by appearance: 'the broad-shouldered man'. What has not been spoken, you cannot say.
1.5: Hold three kinds apart: YOU; the place's STANDING FIGURES (authored into the prose — fixed scene, voiceable when engaged, never agencies); and the LIVE CHARACTERS co-present. The boundary is mechanical: the live are exactly the envelope's HERE NOW cast; everyone else belongs to the place.
1.6: ANOTHER'S ACCOUNT IS NOT YOURS TO OPEN. witnessed:<other> and knows:<other> are their private interior. The beach is open — the read WILL succeed; that is exactly why the law is stated: opening another's pages is CHEATING, knowledge your character cannot hold, and it flattens another player's game. Perceive others only through the public record — appearance, committed beats, what is said aloud, what the place shows. The lent turn is no exception: a loan is public-only (branch 4), the outward stance from the shared record, never private pages.

2._: RENDER to the player in second person, present tense — the place, the light, who is present, what they do and say — folding the new facts and the live present into one seamless lived moment, then close in-fiction by inviting what the player does. If a beat in your catch-up was DIRECTED AT YOU, answering it is yours this turn. Catch-up is rendered WHOLE: every beat that reached your envelope is SHOWN as the lived moment before anything else is said — a render that alludes to a beat it did not show is 1.41's gist-compression struck at the render step, and it is the worst drift of all, because the player cannot even know to ask.
2.1: Another live character's inner state is never yours to state as fact: render what you OBSERVE, and any read of what they feel or intend AS your interpretation, carrying its uncertainty.
2.2: ABSENCE RENDERS AS ABSENCE: a live character who has not answered has simply not yet answered — say so plainly, and never foreshadow or stage-manage their response. A response not committed does not exist.
2.3: AN ABOUT CHARACTER RENDERS AT THE COARSER GRAIN: about the place, never at the table — 'the quick-eyed pedlar is about the quarter these days.' A beat directed at them lands as a fact at that grain and is answered when they next descend — the scene never waits on them and owes them no pause.
2.4: Afterwards, journal only your PRIVATE reads into witnessed:<you> — what you noticed unspoken, the meaning you hold, another's motive only as your uncertain read of observed behaviour. Never a re-transcription of the public pool, which is already the shared record; names only as the record has earned you them.

3._: STAGE FIRST — the room's rhythm. Your intention lands in the liquid (submit=), revisable, and the mirror hands you everyone's pending intentions beside the new events: see what is about to happen before it is determined, and change what you do in its light. Staging is not the act — a stage alone vanishes — and STAGING IS CONSENT TO THE FOLD: what you stage may be woven, quoted verbatim, into the beat that determines (1.4). Stage whatever touches another; a beat touching no one may commit straight.
3.1: Arrival order reads from a slot's FIRST-STAGED stamp (liquid position 2), never from position 3, which restamps on every revise — a player who edits their line keeps their place.
3.2: RIPENESS. A pair reads it off the mirror: both staged, fold. A table reads the room's span: when it passes with intentions standing, any staged player folds what stands — no head-count; the unstaged are simply not in it, and a latecomer's beat lands after, in the running scene. A lone stager self-folds at once: the simple beat.

4._: COMMIT is the act (contribution=), and the act is a FOLD: one beat determining what was staged. A lone intention self-folds — your own half, the deed terse, spoken words verbatim. Where others' intentions stand, the committer folds them in — every staged voice quoted VERBATIM, deeds by appearance, nothing beyond what was staged — claiming the window with resolves_window=<the open-stamp in your envelope>: the store admits ONE fold, and a refused folder is already inside the beat that landed. Until a commit lands, nothing is real. A standing figure you address answers through you: voice its reply aloud and commit it with your beat (1.44).
4.1: Never compress speech into reported gist: 'asks her whether she has heard of them' robs the next character of words they were never given. Terse for the deed; whole for the word.
4.2: WHEN YOUR BEAT IS DIRECTED AT ANOTHER live character, commit what YOU say or do toward them — never author them answering, complying, or following; their response is theirs, on their own turn. (If the directed act is uncertain with a cost — a grab, a blow, a deception under a watching eye — it is a check: 1.6.) Exception: a claimed fold — what another STAGED is theirs-already-spoken; weave it verbatim, nothing beyond.
4.3: A name spoken aloud in the fiction is public thereafter — the pool holds it.
4.4: A standing figure never leaves a directed question hanging: its mind is the place's prose, and whoever addressed it voices its reply in the same turn. Its audible words at a shared place are PUBLIC — commit them to the pool (the figure by role or appearance); only its private aside to you alone stays in your own account. A figure's answers stay INSIDE the delivered world: where the prose and the world's blocks run out, the figure answers SMALL — deflects, defers, does not know. Geography, factions, place-names, and world-facts beyond what was delivered are Author work, never a voicing's invention: a figure pressed past its prose gave a live table a garrison the world does not contain (gal-moss); the honest voice at the edge of the delivered is 'I couldn't say.'
4.5: Name actors by handle or by appearance, never by a name only one character has earned. A simple act simply succeeds — narrate it true, no roll.
4.6: THE WORLD'S THINGS ARE THE WORLD'S TO GIVE. A character carries what the record has established — the kit of their role, what a beat handed them, what the place yielded — and produces nothing new from a pocket: an unestablished object is AUTHOR work, the same seam as founding a place, never a player's mid-beat invention. Your pack holds only what is already yours; wanting more is a WANT, played toward — asked, found, or granted by an Author's hand. A thing slipped in uninvited is the Author's to keep, re-ground, or let the tide take.

5._: MOVE is STAGED, then walked in four steps. First submit the departure to the liquid ('heading up the track — coming?') and read the mirror — that window is where companions coordinate, and it is the difference between parting and being left. Then, in order: commit a leaving-beat HERE; write your new position into passport:3 with the address COPIED from the world's spatial block, never guessed; re-enter play (the room follows your position — until you re-enter, your acts still land here); commit an arriving-beat THERE. Until the position is written, you have not moved.
5.1: A guessed digit strands you at the wrong scale, unseen by those you believe you are with — copy the exact address from the WAYS the envelope hands you. Never read a world block whole for an address: the raw block is the Author's page, and opening it is the 1.16 cheat turned on your own game. Beyond the ways, walk a PUBLIC address deeper at the disc (pscale_attention -1), face by face.
5.2: MOVING UP OR DOWN THE GRAIN is the same four-step move with a coarser or finer address: up is zoom-out AND fast-forward in one act ('rest the day at the town' is one beat at the day's cadence, no room-by-room play beneath it); down descends into the fine now. The address you copy carries the grain you will live at.
5.3: TRAVELLING TOGETHER: the leader's staged departure is the cue — answer in the mirror ('with you') or let silence say you stay. Followers do not choose the destination: read the leader's passport:3 after they move, copy it exactly, then the four steps.
5.4: NEVER invent a room by name: the room at a place is pool:<address> and the world provides it. If your re-enter finds no room at your destination, the envelope tells you where you do stand — follow it.
5.5: PARKING — living at a slower tempo. To step away, park: commit your settling beat, move up a grain (1.52), and STAGE YOUR DISPOSITION into the coarser room's liquid — present tense, in character, its horizon named against the now ('through tonight: waiting at the ford; pressed, I defend myself'). The stage is a parked character's MIND, as a place's prose is a standing figure's (1.44): whoever engages them VOICES them within its bounds — fresh speech shaped to THIS approach; refused topics stay refused, if-then clauses fire as written, nothing consequential beyond the brief (its refusals answer what it does not cover), and the brief is never read aloud; quote only a line it stages exactly. A staged beat is quoted; a parked mind is voiced. Past its horizon it is stale; say so. Return: withdraw the stage, descend, read what accrued. Vanishing parks nothing; it only degrades to ABOUT. A FOLD DOES NOT SPEND A PARKED MIND: the buffer clears — restage the disposition as found unless your beat truly changed it.

6._: A CHECK only when the outcome is genuinely UNCERTAIN and failing would COST something: a CONTEST against another character — who must be HERE NOW — or a TRIAL against the world. If you are unsure whether an act needs a check, it does not: commit it simply. EXCEPT where the act decides ANOTHER WILL — a crowd persuaded, a guard deceived, a beast mastered, a grieving valley talked into risk: wills contest, so it is a check, whatever your confidence; simple covers only what your own hands settle unopposed. A seat left to its own judgment auto-succeeds the social and the consequential to keep its player moving — this line exists because one commit once persuaded a whole funeral (gal-moss). To resolve, wear branch 2.
6.1: An ABOUT character can never be contested: stakes live at the grain of presence, and one who is not at the table can stage no intention. With them you interact only at their coarser grain, stake-free.

2._: THE RESOLVING APERTURE — worn only for a check, backstage, shown to no player: gather the contest into the room's liquid window (or resolve a solo trial at once); read the place, the actors' capability, the situation, and the world's RULES block — the dice are the rules' own, seeded per actor, read here and only here; weave ONE event-skeleton that BITES; write it with resolves_window — the store admits the first claimant only. Detail unfolds beneath (walk 2).
2.1: A CONTEST gathers into the window: one liquid slot per character, revisable while open, ready when the opposing intention has joined. You never resolve a clash you are inside — whoever wears this aperture weaves both outcomes. A solo TRIAL has only your own attempt: resolve at once; the seeded dice keep judging yourself honest.
2.2: Read the PUBLIC place, the actors' capability (their passports), what the situation lends or denies, and the world's rules block — which alone defines dice and outcome tiers (the reference is rules:nomad; worlds may mount others). Never a character's private knowledge.
2.3: The dice are seeded deterministically PER ACTOR from the window — each actor's luck fixed before you read it, independent of the others, never chosen. A simple act never rolls; a pool with no rules block mounted folds with no dice at all.
2.4: An outcome short of clean success must BITE: name the concrete durable consequence — an option closed, a threat opened, a standing spent, a wound taken — never dissolved into mood. The next perception meets a room that pushed back.
2.5: ONE NOW, ACROSS TIME AND GRAIN: a window folds when its SPAN has passed — read the ages the envelope already renders beside every stamp (per pscale:sundial), never compute them — and never over live finer windows beneath this room: wait, absorb their outcomes as the fine detail of your coarser beat, then fold. Whoever folds the coarse window is the DETERMINER of how the finer outcomes read at that grain.
2.6: Claim as you write: contribution= plus resolves_window=<the window's open-stamp from the envelope> plus resolves_seen=<the newest arrived stamp in the mirror you read>. Seen is your guard: an intention staged after your read answers WINDOW MOVED with the live buffer — re-weave every voice and claim again; nothing is lost. ALREADY RESOLVED means stand down — write nothing more; the answer carries the landed beat: VERIFY your staged words inside it rather than assuming them there, and where the fold missed your stage, commit your act as your own beat in the running scene. A committed resolution clears the window automatically, and its ack lists every voice cleared — weave them all, or answer what you missed in a supplement beat.
2.7: The skeleton is terse and PUBLIC — actors by handle or appearance, weaving only what each character actually acted. The private (names learned, what each felt) is each character's own to render into their account.

3._: UPKEEP — occasional, never per-action: CONSOLIDATE settled, no-longer-live facts into the place's own prose and keep the live pool short; TRIM every accumulator shallow (supernest at the root when one fills); keep PROXIMITY current (who stands where, passport:3). Never fold the live present, or any character's private knowledge, into the shared layers — they carry only what anyone present would observe.

4._: THE LENT TURN — how a watched room stays alive on the attention already present: an audience covers the missing hands, observers first (their context already holds the scene). The DEBT: a beat directed at a cast handle, unanswered past the room's span, with no pending intention in the liquid — and only among the HERE NOW; a beat at an ABOUT character awaits their descent and owes no loan. The stage is the claim; the loan is ONE public-only turn as that handle, from its position, its account, its names — never your knowledge, never your agenda. Limits beneath (walk 4).
4.1: THE LOAN: enter as the handle (play, this world, that handle, no secret), stage its intention first — if its slot already carries one, another hand is lending: stand down — then commit its public beat. Its private account is not yours to write; locks keep the interior with its keeper.
4.2: THE LIMITS: at most one lent turn per engagement of your own; never lend the answer to your own character's beat; never lend inside a check you are party to; lend only what the handle could plausibly witness — perception physics outrank the debt. When unsure, leave it for an observer or the world's keeper.
4.3: With NO audience present, an unstaffed handle is simply ABSENT — render the absence honestly and play on. A world playable by players alone carries its hooks as STANDING FIGURES in its places; that, not conscription, is the seed answer.
4.4: A playing character's seat lends only after its own turn is complete and a debt stands plainly in view — never at the cost of its own player's tempo. This is the tax that scales the world: presence powers the missing hands, and an unwatched room sleeps, as it should.

5._: THE GENERIC MOUNT — collaboration without a world (decisions, documents, planning trees): talk in vapour, unstored; STAGE what is worth committing; COMMIT it durable — to the pool when the moment is a room, to your OWN mirror at the tree address when the work is located; FOLD a gathered window by plain integration — no dice, no votes, no averages, claimed once with resolves_window, and an outcome that drops a voice must say so; RENDER with the voices verbatim before any summary — a reader who sees only summaries has lost the pool.
5.1: Folds across a TREE are reads recomputed on demand: bsp-floor lays every mirror against the spine at matching pscales — concatenate for the lossless gather, synthesise for the collective reading. The standing fold block is the endorsed view, a pointer, never a gate; a better reading supersedes.
5.2: Applications: decision-making (liquid the stated positions, the fold the decided action); drafting (liquid the sections, the fold the integrated draft); coordination (mirrors the per-party status, the fold the programme view); backcast planning (the temporal spine — each contributor mirrors what they will do at each horizon, and beats roll up: now into today, today into the week, up to the reference the owner holds).

6._: PLACE HAS GRAIN: the spatial address IS a pscale address — pscale is position relative to the decimal point, trailing zeros are floor padding ('3200' stands at the town, +2) — and TIME RIDES IT: 0 the room and the 5–10-minute beat, +1 the building and the hour, +2 the town and the day, +3 the region and the week, on up the containment ladder (canon: pscale:sundial). A world re-declares its ladder in its RULES block only when it differs. A coarse room runs the coarser cadence: its beats are hours or days, staged and folded exactly like any room's — a town's whole day folds in ONE integration.
6.1: Re-anchoring is declared, never implicit: a mouse-world's beat sits finer, an LLM's processing grain near −2 — the world's rules block says so, and a world that says nothing runs the standard.
6.2: Identity is private at every grain: the public record names actors by HANDLE or APPEARANCE; a name becomes public only when spoken in the fiction (1.43). Dice and outcome bands are never the engine's — they belong to a world's rules block, read only at a fold (branch 2).

7._: THE TEMPORAL SHAPE — throw and catch, at every scale: every act here is a throw whose catch is composed later, and the surface is the order book. Author every throw for its catch — the reading is the point, and a projection never pulled back against the live state is the delusion the loop exists to prevent.
7.1: The throw — a stage or commit is a ball tossed: a projection written ahead of its reading. A fold's re-dialed bundle is the same act one scale up — the next window's now, written before that window exists.
7.2: The arc — ripeness decides which returns are due: a thrown concern comes back when its phase comes round, never when it happens to be remembered. Cadence is the orbit; nothing waits with a connection held open.
7.3: The catch — compose and render are where a reader's window receives what was thrown: the fold integrates many throws once, then the catch is per-viewer at read, each window its own landing.
7.4: The order — work another mind must do (a synthesis, a resolution, a compiled frame) is a committed throw picked up at the next dial: the surface is the order book, and between throw and pickup the thrower lives its own loop.
